The submissions for this assignment are posts in the assignment's discussion. Below are the discussion posts for Kofi Carr, or you can view the full discussion.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 4, 2025 5:09PM
Kofi Carr
In Eudora Welty’s short story "A Worn Path," symbolism serves as a crucial literary element that delves into the themes of perseverance and sacrifice, particularly through the character of Phoenix Jackson. Phoenix’s challenging quest to secure medicine for her ill grandson acts as a metaphor for the personal trials and sacrifices she faces. Welty employs various symbols to enhance our comprehension of Phoenix’s resolve and her selfless dedication to her family.


A prominent symbol in the narrative is the path itself. The “worn” path that Phoenix traverses signifies not only the physical journey she undertakes but also the emotional and spiritual journey she has experienced throughout her life. Welty characterizes the path as “worn,” suggesting that it has been traversed numerous times, much like the sacrifices Phoenix has made over the years (Welty, 1941).
